title: Understanding the Forces
text: To find the maximum distance the man can stand without sliding, consider the forces acting on him. The static frictional force must be equal to the centripetal force required to keep him moving in a circle.

title: Centripetal Force Formula
text: The centripetal force needed to keep the man moving in a circle is given by the formula: $$F_c=$$ $$m r {\omega}^2$$, where $$m$$ is the mass, $$r$$ is the radius, and omega is the angular velocity.

title: Static Friction Force
text: The maximum static frictional force that can act on the man is given by: $$F_f=$$ $$\mu_s m g$$, where $$\mu_s$$ is the coefficient of static friction, $$m$$ is the mass, and g is the acceleration due to gravity.

title: Equating Forces
text: Set the maximum static frictional force equal to the centripetal force to find the maximum radius: $$\mu_s m g=m r {\omega}^2$$. Solve for $$r$$.

title: Substitute Values
text: Substitute the given values into the equation: $$\mu_s=0.5$$, $$g=9.8$$, $$omega=2.5$$. Calculate the maximum distance $$r$$.
